what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block that proposes a verb-first taxonomy for editorial marks (Change, Query, Note, Approve, Strike) and argues that the gesture or long-press UI should classify the mark type before any text is entered.
observations: It defines five mark verbs: Change, Query, Comment/Note, Approve/Stet, and Strike/Reject.
It claims the mark type is the first decision an editor makes, not an optional after-the-fact intent tag.
It proposes a long-press radial menu that resolves to verbs first, with distinct icons, using DNA brown-circle iconography.
It argues that many marks (e.g., Approve, Strike) should be one tap with zero typing.
It links each verb to routing/triage: Query routes to an answerer, Change routes to an editor, Approve resolves a thread.
It references redlining symbols (caret, loop, wavy underline) and contrasts them with chatty tools.
It references BD-D INTENT and BD-E supervisor triage without defining them in the file.
It has no code symbols and links to board://item-a72b23c2 as part_of.
standouts: Verb-first taxonomy as the primary gesture decision.
One-tap Approve/Strike with no typing required.
Verb type used as a routing key for envelope triage.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML frontmatter convention with id, address, type, links, and history.
Uses editorial/proofreading terminology and symbolic mark conventions.
Breaks from comment-first UI patterns by prioritizing mark classification over typed text.
concerns: The title appears truncated, ending with 'missing the ta'.
References such as BD-D, BD-E, Tim, and DNA brown-circle iconography are undefined in this file.
The design claims are prescriptive and not supported by data within the file.
notes: A design argument that editorial mark gestures should start with a fixed verb taxonomy because the verb determines routing and many marks need no text.
questions: What do BD-D and BD-E refer to?
What is the 'DNA' brown-circle iconography system?
What is the parent item board://item-a72b23c2?
How would the proposed radial verbs map to the existing optional intent tag in the data model?
What does 'P2 · 1' signify in the title?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to argue for a verb-first taxonomy for editorial marks; the file does exactly that, though the title is truncated.
intention_signals: It is trying to shift the design from a generic comment/intent-tag model to a structured, symbol-driven editor-mark vocabulary where the verb is the primary gesture and the routing key.
novelty: It explicitly treats editorial mark verbs as both UI gestures and routing keys, proposing zero-typing approvals and strikes.
contribution: It contributes a concrete five-verb taxonomy and a verb-first radial-gesture proposal that could replace or augment an optional intent tag, plus a routing rationale tied to mark type.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design note arguing that editorial mark gestures should classify the mark into one of five verbs (Change, Query, Note, Approve, Strike) before any text is entered, because the verb drives routing and many marks need no words.
intention_for_embedding: To persuade the design team to adopt a verb-first taxonomy and radial gesture for editorial marks, using mark type as a routing key.
